r, r, a, y, s]"},{"id":1071716919,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMDcxNzE2OTE5","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/needs%20pkgeval","name":"needs change"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":54,"created_at":"2024-09-28T05:08:09Z","updated_at":"2024-12-04T23:09:30Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/55913","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55913","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55913.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55913.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"This speeds up making new `Memory`s and allow the compiler to better understand what's going on, allowing for LLVM level escape analysis in some cases. There is more room to grow this (currently this only optimizes for fairly small `Memory` since bigger ones would require writing some more LLVM code, and we probably want a size limit on putting `Memory` on the stack to avoid stackoverflow. For larger ones, we could potentially inline the `free` so the `Memory` doesn't have to be swept by the GC, etc. 

Benchmarks:
```
julia> f(T) = Memory{T}(undef, 2);

julia> @btime f(Int);
  7.879 ns (1 allocation: 48 bytes) #before
  3.959 ns (1 allocation: 48 bytes) #after 

julia> @btime f(Union{Int, Nothing})
  8.426 ns (1 allocation: 48 bytes) #before
  4.017 ns (1 allocation: 48 bytes) #after 

julia> @btime f(Any);
  8.637 ns (1 allocation: 48 bytes) #before
  3.920 ns (1 allocation: 48 bytes) #after 

julia> function g()
           m = Memory{Int}(undef, 2)
           for i in 1:2
              m[i] = i
           end
           m[1]+m[2]
       end

julia> @btime g()
  9.735 ns (1 allocation: 48 bytes) #before
  1.719 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes) #after
```

r, r, a, y, s]"},{"id":1071716919,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMDcxNzE2OTE5","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/needs%20pkgeval","name":"needs change"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":71,"created_at":"2024-09-28T05:08:09Z","updated_at":"2024-12-06T10:29:29Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/55913","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55913","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55913.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55913.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"This speeds up making new `Memory`s and allow the compiler to better understand what's going on, allowing for LLVM level escape analysis in some cases. There is more room to grow this (currently this only optimizes for fairly small `Memory` since bigger ones would require writing some more LLVM code, and we probably want a size limit on putting `Memory` on the stack to avoid stackoverflow. For larger ones, we could potentially inline the `free` so the `Memory` doesn't have to be swept by the GC, etc. 

Benchmarks:
```
julia> f(T) = Memory{T}(undef, 2);

julia> @btime f(Int);
  7.879 ns (1 allocation: 48 bytes) #before
  3.959 ns (1 allocation: 48 bytes) #after 

julia> @btime f(Union{Int, Nothing})
  8.426 ns (1 allocation: 48 bytes) #before
  4.017 ns (1 allocation: 48 bytes) #after 

julia> @btime f(Any);
  8.637 ns (1 allocation: 48 bytes) #before
  3.920 ns (1 allocation: 48 bytes) #after 

julia> function g()
           m = Memory{Int}(undef, 2)
           for i in 1:2
              m[i] = i
           end
           m[1]+m[2]
       end

julia> @btime g()
  9.735 ns (1 allocation: 48 bytes) #before
  1.719 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes) #after
```

PRs:
- [x] #56640 <!-- subtype: fast path for Type == TypeVar -->
- [x] #56789 <!-- Fix test report alignment -->
- [x] #56784 <!-- fix `exp(weirdNaN)` -->
- [x] #56317 <!-- Fix generate_precompile statement grouping & avoid defining new func -->
- [x] #56787 <!-- Fix partially_inline for unreachable -->

Need manual backport:
- [ ] #54634 <!-- Fix trampoline assembly for build on clang 18 on apple silicon -->
- [ ] #55908 <!-- add logic to prefer loading modules that are already loaded -->
- [ ] #56097 <!-- rearrange jl_delete_thread to be thread-safe -->
- [ ] #56264 <!-- inference: fix inference error from constructing invalid `TypeVar` -->
- [ ] #56306 <!-- InteractiveUtils.jl: fixes issue where subtypes resolves bindings and causes deprecation warnings  -->
- [ ] #56329 <!-- loading: clean up more concurrency issues -->
- [ ] #56391 <!-- irinterp: set `IR_FLAG_REFINED` for narrowed `PhiNode`s -->
- [ ] #56236 <!-- Allow taking Matrix slices without an extra allocation -->
- [ ] #55223 <!-- count bytes allocated through malloc more precisely -->
- [ ] #55871 <!-- lowering: don't reverse handler order in `(pop-handler-list ...)` -->
- [ ] #56749 <!-- precompile: don't waste memory on useless inferred code -->
- [ ] #56801 <!-- gc: improve mallocarrays locality -->

Contains multiple commits, manual intervention needed:
- [ ] #55877 <!-- fix FileWatching designs and add workaround for a stat bug on Apple -->
- [ ] #54457 <!-- Make `String(::Memory)` copy -->
- [ ] #42080 <!-- recommend explicit `using Foo: Foo, ...` in package code (was: "using considered harmful") -->
- [ ] #56370 <!-- precompile: fix performance issues with IO -->
- [ ] #56644 <!-- fix jl_mutex_lock deadlock under rr -->
- [ ] #56625 <!-- codegen: make more judicious use of global rooting -->

Non-merged PRs with backport label:
- [ ] #56808 <!-- Export opaque closure constructor -->
- [ ] #56794 <!-- Make `Pairs` public -->
- [ ] #56793 <!-- Extend `Base.rationalize` instead of defining new function `Base.MathConstants.rationalize` -->
- [ ] #56709 <!-- Update version that version-named manifests works from -->
- [ ] #56423 <!-- Fix and test lcm([1//2, 1//2]) == 1//1 -->
- [ ] #56411 <!-- xref `UnionAll` in the doc string of `where` -->
- [ ] #56281 <!-- REPL: complete imports when there are multiple macros before -->
- [ ] #55958 <!-- also redirect JL_STDERR etc. when redirecting to devnull -->
- [ ] #55956 <!-- Make threadcall gc safe -->
- [ ] #55813 <!-- Check for conflicting `@ccallable` name before JIT registration -->
- [ ] #55534 <!-- Set stdlib sources as read-only during installation -->
- [ ] #55499 <!-- propagate the terminal's `displaysize` to the `IOContext` used by the REPL -->
- [ ] #55458 <!-- Allow for generically extracting unannotated string -->
- [ ] #55457 <!-- Make AnnotateChar equality consider annotations -->
- [ ] #55220 <!-- `isfile_casesensitive` fixes on Windows -->
- [ ] #55169 <!-- `propertynames` for SVD respects private argument -->
- [ ] #53957 <!-- tweak how filtering is done for what packages should be precompiled -->
- [ ] #51479 <!-- prevent code loading from lookin in the versioned environment when building Julia -->
- [ ] #50813 <!-- More doctests for Sockets and capitalization fix -->

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56851","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56851/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56851/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56851/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56851","id":2745292825,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM6Fg0uA","number":56851,"title":"Compiler.jl: use `Base.[all|any]` instead of `Compiler`'s own versions","user":{"login":"aviatesk","id":40514306,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwNTE0MzA2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/40514306?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk","html_url":"https://github.com/aviatesk","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-12-17T15:48:52Z","updated_at":"2024-12-18T01:00:31Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56851","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56851","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56851.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56851.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"The current `Compiler` defines its own versions of `all` and `any`, which are separate generic functions from `Base.[all|any]`: https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/2ed1a411e0a080f3107e75bb65105a15a0533a90/Compiler/src/utilities.jl#L15-L32

On the other hand, at the point where `Base.Compiler` is bootstrapped, only a subset of `Base.[all|any]` are defined, specifically those related to `Tuple`:
https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/2ed1a411e0a080f3107e75bb65105a15a0533a90/base/tuple.jl#L657-L668. Consequently, in the type inference world, functions like `Base.all(::Generator)` are unavailable. If `Base.Compiler` attempts to perform operations such as `::BitSet ⊆ ::BitSet` (which internally uses `Base.[all|any]`), a world age error occurs (while `Compiler.[all|any]` can handle these operations, `::BitSet ⊆ ::BitSet` uses `Base.[all|any]`, leading to this issue)

To resolve this problem, this commit removes the custom `Compiler` versions of `[all|any]` and switches to using the Base versions.

One concern is that the previous `Compiler` versions of `[all|any]` utilized `@nospecialize`. However, since this was introduced a long time ago, I believe it does not cause any issues in the current implementation of the Compiler.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56851/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56851/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2550032393","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56851#issuecomment-2550032393","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56851","id":2550032393,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6X_mgJ","user":{"login":"nanosoldier","id":14142636,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0MTQyNjM2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/14142636?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier","html_url":"https://github.com/nanosoldier","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-18T01:00:30Z","updated_at":"2024-12-18T01:00:30Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"[Your benchmark job](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56851#issuecomment-2549109918) has completed - https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55913 (the rebase was more annoying than starting from scratch)
This allows the compiler to better understand what's going on for `memorynew` with compile-time constant length, allowing for LLVM level escape analysis in some cases. There is more room to grow this (currently this only optimizes for fairly small Memory since bigger ones would require writing some more LLVM code, and we probably want a size limit on putting Memory on the stack to avoid stackoverflow. For larger ones, we could potentially inline the free so the Memory doesn't have to be swept by the GC, etc.
```
julia> function g()
           m = Memory{Int}(undef, 2)
           for i in 1:2
              m[i] = i
           end
           m[1]+m[2]
       end

julia> @btime g()
  9.735 ns (1 allocation: 48 bytes) #before
  1.719 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes) #after
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56847/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56847/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2552403464","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56847#issuecomment-2552403464","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56847","id":2552403464,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6YIpYI","user":{"login":"nanosoldier","id":14142636,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0MTQyNjM2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/14142636?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier","html_url":"https://github.com/nanosoldier","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-18T22:38:32Z","updated_at":"2024-12-18T22:38:32Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"The package evaluation job [you requested](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56847#issuecomment-2547472640) has completed - possible new issues were detected.

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56851","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56851/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56851/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56851/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56851","id":2745292825,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM6Fg0uA","number":56851,"title":"Compiler.jl: use `Base.[all|any]` instead of `Compiler`'s own versions","user":{"login":"aviatesk","id":40514306,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwNTE0MzA2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/40514306?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk","html_url":"https://github.com/aviatesk","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":5,"created_at":"2024-12-17T15:48:52Z","updated_at":"2024-12-19T00:01:06Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56851","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56851","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56851.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56851.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"The current `Compiler` defines its own versions of `all` and `any`, which are separate generic functions from `Base.[all|any]`: https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/2ed1a411e0a080f3107e75bb65105a15a0533a90/Compiler/src/utilities.jl#L15-L32

On the other hand, at the point where `Base.Compiler` is bootstrapped, only a subset of `Base.[all|any]` are defined, specifically those related to `Tuple`:
https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/2ed1a411e0a080f3107e75bb65105a15a0533a90/base/tuple.jl#L657-L668. Consequently, in the type inference world, functions like `Base.all(::Generator)` are unavailable. If `Base.Compiler` attempts to perform operations such as `::BitSet ⊆ ::BitSet` (which internally uses `Base.[all|any]`), a world age error occurs (while `Compiler.[all|any]` can handle these operations, `::BitSet ⊆ ::BitSet` uses `Base.[all|any]`, leading to this issue)

To resolve this problem, this commit removes the custom `Compiler` versions of `[all|any]` and switches to using the Base versions.

One concern is that the previous `Compiler` versions of `[all|any]` utilized `@nospecialize`. https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55913 (the rebase was more annoying than starting from scratch)
This allows the compiler to better understand what's going on for `memorynew` with compile-time constant length, allowing for LLVM level escape analysis in some cases. There is more room to grow this (currently this only optimizes for fairly small Memory since bigger ones would require writing some more LLVM code, and we probably want a size limit on putting Memory on the stack to avoid stackoverflow. For larger ones, we could potentially inline the free so the Memory doesn't have to be swept by the GC, etc.
```
julia> function g()
           m = Memory{Int}(undef, 2)
           for i in 1:2
              m[i] = i
           end
           m[1]+m[2]
       end

julia> @btime g()
  9.735 ns (1 allocation: 48 bytes) #before
  1.719 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes) #after
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56847/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56847/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2555220282","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56847#issuecomment-2555220282","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56847","id":2555220282,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6YTZE6","user":{"login":"nanosoldier","id":14142636,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0MTQyNjM2","avatar_url":"https://private-avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/14142636?jwt=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJnaXRodWIuY29tIiwiYXVkIjoicmF3LmdpdGh1YnVzZXJjb250ZW50LmNvbSIsImtleSI6ImtleTEiLCJleHAiOjE3MzQ2Mjk3NjAsIm5iZiI6MTczNDYyODU2MCwicGF0aCI6Ii91LzE0MTQyNjM2In0.QDdl2MyniBQr9gA1OJAPs9E6PEXAsclJc049Rq9YCN0&v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier","html_url":"https://github.com/nanosoldier","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-19T17:19:22Z","updated_at":"2024-12-19T17:19:22Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"[Your {"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56851","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56851/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56851/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56851/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56851","id":2745292825,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM6Fg0uA","number":56851,"title":"Compiler.jl: use `Base.[all|any]` instead of `Compiler`'s own versions","user":{"login":"aviatesk","id":40514306,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwNTE0MzA2","avatar_url":"https://private-avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/40514306?jwt=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJnaXRodWIuY29tIiwiYXVkIjoicmF3LmdpdGh1YnVzZXJjb250ZW50LmNvbSIsImtleSI6ImtleTEiLCJleHAiOjE3MzQ2MzM4NDAsIm5iZiI6MTczNDYzMjY0MCwicGF0aCI6Ii91LzQwNTE0MzA2In0.ges9SWraiOHLXkhtac70_y9yx2PPDUncg22hsB-r_nM&v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk","html_url":"https://github.com/aviatesk","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":7,"created_at":"2024-12-17T15:48:52Z","updated_at":"2024-12-19T18:25:20Z","closed_at":"2024-12-19T06:23:21Z","author_association":"MEMBER","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56851","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56851","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56851.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56851.patch","merged_at":"2024-12-19T06:23:20Z"},"body":"The current `Compiler` defines its own versions of `all` and `any`, which are separate generic functions from `Base.[all|any]`: https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/2ed1a411e0a080f3107e75bb65105a15a0533a90/Compiler/src/utilities.jl#L15-L32

On the other hand, at the point where `Base.Compiler` is bootstrapped, only a subset of `Base.[all|any]` are defined, specifically those related to `Tuple`:
https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/2ed1a411e0a080f3107e75bb65105a15a0533a90/base/tuple.jl#L657-L668. Consequently, in the type inference world, functions like `Base.all(::Generator)` are unavailable. If `Base.Compiler` attempts to perform operations such as `::BitSet ⊆ ::BitSet` (which internally uses `Base.[all|any]`), a world age error occurs (while `Compiler.[all|any]` can handle these operations, `::BitSet ⊆ ::BitSet` uses `Base.[all|any]`, leading to this issue)

To resolve this problem, this commit removes the custom `Compiler` versions of `[all|any]` and switches to using the Base versions.

~~One concern is that the previous `Compiler` versions of `[all|any]` utilized `@nospecialize`. However, since this was introduced a long time ago, I believe it does not cause any issues in the current implementation of the Compiler.~~
EDIT: One concern is that the previous `Compiler` versions of `[all|any]`
utilized `@nospecialize`. That annotation was introduced a long time ago
to prevent over-specialization, but it is questionable whether it is
still effective with the current compiler implementation. The results of
the nanosoldier benchmarks conducted below also seem to confirm that
the `@nospecialize`s are no longer necessary for those functions.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56851/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56851/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2555506447","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56851#issuecomment-2555506447","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56851","id":2555506447,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6YUe8P","user":{"login":"nanosoldier","id":14142636,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0MTQyNjM2","avatar_url":"https://private-avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/14142636?jwt=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJnaXRodWIuY29tIiwiYXVkIjoicmF3LmdpdGh1YnVzZXJjb250ZW50LmNvbSIsImtleSI6ImtleTEiLCJleHAiOjE3MzQ2MzMzNjAsIm5iZiI6MTczNDYzMjE2MCwicGF0aCI6Ii91LzE0MTQyNjM2In0.hiGQKMKgjDW9sqUnfpykE1Wy9CdZTynJIJwvVar7FLU&v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier","html_url":"https://github.com/nanosoldier","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-19T18:25:20Z","updated_at":"2024-12-19T18:25:20Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"[Your https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55913 (the rebase was more annoying than starting from scratch)
This allows the compiler to better understand what's going on for `memorynew` with compile-time constant length, allowing for LLVM level escape analysis in some cases. There is more room to grow this (currently this only optimizes for fairly small Memory since bigger ones would require writing some more LLVM code, and we probably want a size limit on putting Memory on the stack to avoid stackoverflow. For larger ones, we could potentially inline the free so the Memory doesn't have to be swept by the GC, etc.
```
julia> function g()
           m = Memory{Int}(undef, 2)
           for i in 1:2
              m[i] = i
           end
           m[1]+m[2]
       end

julia> @btime g()
  9.735 ns (1 allocation: 48 bytes) #before
  1.719 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes) #after
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56847/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56847/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2560416475","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56847#issuecomment-2560416475","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56847","id":2560416475,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6YnNrb","user":{"login":"nanosoldier","id":14142636,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0MTQyNjM2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/14142636?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier","html_url":"https://github.com/nanosoldier","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-23T23:12:59Z","updated_at":"2024-12-23T23:12:59Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"The package evaluation job [you requested](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56847#issuecomment-2560372871) has completed - possible new issues were detected.

inference using optimizer-derived type information","user":{"login":"aviatesk","id":40514306,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwNTE0MzA2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/40514306?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk","html_url":"https://github.com/aviatesk","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2024-11-26T16:30:42Z","updated_at":"2024-12-26T17:43:29Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56687","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56687","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56687.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56687.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"In certain cases, the optimizer can introduce new type information. This is particularly evident in SROA, where load forwarding can reveal type information that was not visible during abstract interpretation. In such cases, re-running abstract interpretation using this new type information can be highly valuable, however, currently, this only occurs when semi-concrete interpretation happens to be triggered.

This commit introduces a new "post-optimization inference" phase at the end of the optimizer pipeline. When the optimizer derives new type information, this phase performs IR abstract interpretation to further optimize the IR.

Such cases are especially common in patterns involving captured
variables, as discussed in JuliaLang/julia#15276. By combining the
"post-optimization inference" implemented in this commit with EA-based
load forwarding, it is anticipated that the issue described in
JuliaLang/julia#15276 can largely be resolved.

---

@nanosoldier `runbenchmarks("inference", vs=":master")`

See descriptions in the individual commits for some of the specifics of the changes and fixes, and how to change existing code to use these API correctly. In followup stages, most code relevant to precompile_utils, trim, and even the allocation-checker should consider being moved into Julia also now, since being written in C/C++ is currently providing negative value for maintaining those, and the change in the API boundary should now make that additional conversion easier.

Gives a considerably smaller system image, despite having more code, by being better algorithms which avoid allocating permanent garbage: 155 MB -> 147MB in `.text`

Makes a slightly larger Pkg.ji file cache, hopefully mainly due to being more strategic about what code is compiled (because that logic is mostly now in Julia instead of C), as it appear to have both inferred and compiled about 10% more code according to high level analysis of it:
$ du -sh usr/share/julia/compiled/v1.12/
237M    # on PR

commit teaches to compiler to update its world bounds whenever it looks at a binding partition, making the compiler sound in the presence of a partitioned binding. The key adjustment is that the compiler is no longer allowed to directly query the binding table without recording the world bounds, so all the various abstract evaluations that look at bindings need to be adjusted and are no longer pure tfuncs. We used to look at bindings a lot more, but thanks to earlier prep work to remove unnecessary binding-dependent code (#55288, #55289 and #55271), these changes become relatively straightforward.\r\n\r\nNote that as before, we do not create any binding partitions by default, so this commit is mostly preperatory.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56299/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":1,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56299/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2451049773","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56299#issuecomment-2451049773","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56299","id":2451049773,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6SGA0t","user":{"login":"nanosoldier","id":14142636,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0MTQyNjM2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/14142636?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier","html_url":"https://github.com/nanosoldier","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-01T00:10:45Z","updated_at":"2024-11-01T00:10:45Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"[Your commit teaches to compiler to update its world bounds whenever it looks at a binding partition, making the compiler sound in the presence of a partitioned binding. The key adjustment is that the compiler is no longer allowed to directly query the binding table without recording the world bounds, so all the various abstract evaluations that look at bindings need to be adjusted and are no longer pure tfuncs. We used to look at bindings a lot more, but thanks to earlier prep work to remove unnecessary binding-dependent code (#55288, #55289 and #55271), these changes become relatively straightforward.\r\n\r\nNote that as before, we do not create any binding partitions by default, so this commit is mostly PRs:
- [x] #50832 <!-- Subtype: bug fix for bounds with deeper covariant var -->
- [x] #51782 <!-- Fix remove-addrspaces pass in the presence of globals with addrspaces -->
- [x] #55720 <!-- Fix `pkgdir` for extensions -->
- [x] #55773 <!-- Add compat entry for `Base.donotdelete` -->
- [x] #55886 <!-- irrationals: restrict assume effects annotations to known types -->
- [x] #55867 <!-- update `hash` doc string: `widen` not required any more -->
- [x] #56148 <!-- Make loading work when stdlib deps are missing in the manifest -->
- [x] #55870 <!-- fix infinite recursion in `promote_type` for `Irrational` -->
- [x] #56252 <!-- REPL: fix brace detection when ' is used for transpose -->
- [x] #56264 <!-- inference: fix inference error from constructing invalid `TypeVar` -->
- [x] #56276 <!-- move time_imports and trace_* macros to Base but remain owned by InteractiveUtils -->
- [x] #56254 <!-- REPL: don't complete str and cmd macros when the input matches the internal name like `r_` to `r"` -->
- [x] #56280 <!-- Fix trampoline warning on x86 as well -->
- [x] #56304 <!-- typeintersect: more fastpath to skip intersect under circular env -->
- [x] #56306 <!-- InteractiveUtils.jl: fixes issue where subtypes resolves bindings and causes deprecation warnings  -->
- [x] #42080 <!-- recommend explicit `using Foo: Foo, ...` in package code (was: "using considered harmful") -->

Need manual backport:
- [ ] #55299 <!-- typeintersect: fix bounds merging during inner `intersect_all`. -->
- [ ] #55405 <!-- handle unbound vars in NTuple fields -->
- [ ] #55744 <!-- fix #45494, error in ssa conversion with complex type decl -->
- [ ] #55890 <!-- Profile: fix order of fields in heapsnapshot & improve formatting -->

Contains multiple commits, manual intervention needed:
- [ ] #54009 <!-- allow extensions to trigger from packages in [deps] -->
- [ ] #54303 <!-- LinearAlgebra: improve type-inference in Symmetric/Hermitian matmul -->
- [ ] #52694 <!-- Reinstate similar for AbstractQ for backward compatibility -->
- [ ] #54738 <!-- serialization: fix relocatability bug -->
- [ ] #55052 <!-- Fix `(l/r)mul!` with `Diagonal`/`Bidiagonal` -->
- [ ] #55251 <!-- Restrict binary ops for Diagonal and Symmetric to Number eltypes -->
- [ ] #55306 <!-- AllocOpt: Fix stack lowering where alloca continas boxed and unboxed data -->
- [ ] #55729 <!-- Avoid confounding compilation side effects of `@time_imports` -->
- [ ] #55589 <!-- prevent loading other extensions when precompiling an extension -->
- [ ] #55877 <!-- fix FileWatching designs and add workaround for a stat bug on Apple -->
- [ ] #56234 <!-- Revert "Extensions: make loading of extensions independent of what packages are in the sysimage (#52841) -->
- [ ] #56311 <!-- Fix `log_quasitriu` for internal scaling `s=0` -->
- [ ] #56369 <!-- cache the `find_all_in_cache_path` call during parallel precompilation -->

Non-merged PRs with backport label:
- [ ] #55958 <!-- also redirect JL_STDERR etc. when redirecting to devnull -->
- [ ] #55956 <!-- Make threadcall gc safe -->
- [ ] #55910 <!-- Prevent extensions from blocking parallel pre-compilation -->
- [ ] #55813 <!-- Check for conflicting `@ccallable` name before JIT registration -->
- [ ] #55534 <!-- Set stdlib sources as read-only during installation -->
- [ ] #55499 <!-- propagate the terminal's `displaysize` to the `IOContext` used by the REPL -->
- [ ] #55311 <!-- docs: Add headers for easier Core, Base and stdlib reference -->
- [ ] #55220 <!-- `isfile_casesensitive` fixes on Windows -->
- [ ] #55169 <!-- `propertynames` for SVD respects private argument -->
- [ ] #51479 <!-- prevent code loading from lookin in the versioned environment when building Julia -->
- [ ] #50813 <!-- More doctests for Sockets and capitalization fix -->

- [x] #56251 <!-- REPL: run repl hint generation for modeswitch chars when not switching -->
- [x] #56092 <!-- stream: fix reading LibuvStream into array -->

- [x] #56214 <!-- fix precompile process flags -->

changing Array to use Memory as the backing, we had the option of making non-Vector arrays more flexible, but had instead preserved the restriction that they must be zero offset and equal in length to effectively reverts #36121 and replaces it with #36111, which was the originally proposed alternative to fix #36104. To recap, the question is what should happen for
```
module Foo
    struct F
        v::Foo.F
    end
end
```
i.e. where the type reference tries to refer to the newly defined type via its global path. In #36121 we adjusted things so that we first assign the type to its global binding and then evaluate the field type (leaving the type in an incomplete state in the meantime). The primary reason that this choice was that we would have to deal with incomplete types assigned to global bindings anyway if we ever did #32658. However, I think this was the wrong choice. There is a difference between allowing incomplete types and semantically forcing incomplete types to be globally observable every time a new type is defined.

The situation was a little different four years ago, but with more extensive threading (which can observe the incompletely constructed type) and the upcoming completion of bindings partition, the situation is different. For bindings partition in particular, this would require two invalidations on re-definition, one to the new incomplete type and then back to the complete type. I don't think this is worth it, for the (somewhat niche and possibly-should-be- deprecated-future) case of refering to incompletely defined types by their global names.

So let's instead try the hack in #36111, which does a frontend rewrite of the global path. This should be sufficient to at least address the obvious cases.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56497/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56497/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2466055057","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56497#issuecomment-2466055057","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56497","id":2466055057,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6S_QOR","user":{"login":"nanosoldier","id":14142636,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0MTQyNjM2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/14142636?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier","html_url":"https://github.com/nanosoldier","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-09T05:15:50Z","updated_at":"2024-11-09T05:15:50Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"The package evaluation job [you requested](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56497#issuecomment-2463761843) has completed - possible new issues were detected.

PR introduces a new, toplevel-only, syntax form `:worldinc` that semantically represents the effect of raising the current task's world age to the latest world for the remainder of the current toplevel evaluation (that context being an entry to `eval` or a module expression). For detailed motivation on why this is desirable, see #55145, which I won't repeat here, but the gist is that we never really defined when world-age increments and worse are inconsistent about it. This is something we need to figure out now, because the bindings partition work will make world age even more observable via bindings.

Having created a mechanism for world age increments, the big question is one of policy, i.e. when should these world age increments be inserted.

Several reasonable options exist:
1. After world-age affecting syntax constructs (as proprosed in #55145)
2. Option 1 + some reasonable additional cases that people rely on
3. Before any top level `call` expression
4. Before any expression at toplevel whatsover

As an example, case, consider `a == a` at toplevel. Depending on the semantics that could either be the same as in local scope, or each of the four world age dependent lookups (three binding lookups, one method lookup) could (potentially) occur in a different world age.

The general tradeoff here is between the risk of exposing the user to confusing world age errors and our ability to optimize top-level code (in general, any `:worldinc` statement will require us to fully pessimize or recompile all following code).

This PR basically implements option 2 with the following semantics:

1. The interpreter explicit raises the world age only at `:worldinc` exprs or after `:module` exprs.
2. The frontend inserts `:worldinc` after all struct definitions, method definitions, `using` and `import.
3. The `@eval` macro inserts a worldinc following the call to `eval` if at toplevel
4. A literal (syntactic) call to `include` gains an implicit `worldinc`.

Of these the fourth is probably the most questionable, but is necessary to make this non-breaking for most code patterns. Perhaps it would have been better to make `include` a macro from the beginning (esp because it already has semantics that look a little like reaching into the calling module), but that ship has sailed.

Unfortunately, I don't see any good intermediate options between this PR and option #3 above. I think option #3 is closest to what we have right now, but if we were to choose it and actually fix the soundness issues, I expect that we would be destroying all performance of global-scope code. For this reason, I would like to try to make the version in this PR work, even if the semantics are a little ugly.

The biggest pattern that this PR does not catch is:
```
eval(:(f() = 1))
f()
```

We could apply the same `include` special case to eval, but given the existence of `@eval` which allows addressing this at the macro level, I decided not to. We can decide which way we want to go on this based on what the package ecosystem looks like.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56509/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56509/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2466568097","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56509#issuecomment-2466568097","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56509","id":2466568097,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6TBNeh","user":{"login":"nanosoldier","id":14142636,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0MTQyNjM2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/14142636?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier","html_url":"https://github.com/nanosoldier","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-10T03:37:42Z","updated_at":"2024-11-10T03:37:42Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"The package evaluation job [you requested](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56509#issuecomment-2466116701) has completed - possible new issues were detected.

closures","user":{"login":"aviatesk","id":40514306,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwNTE0MzA2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/40514306?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk","html_url":"https://github.com/aviatesk","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-11-12T16:55:21Z","updated_at":"2024-11-12T18:01:01Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56532","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56532","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56532.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56532.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"As an idea to improve type stability for capturing closures, such as in JuliaLang/julia#31909, I tried this idea of propagating the closure object as a `PartialStruct` whose `fields` include captured variables of which types are (partially) known. By performing const-prop on this `closure::PartialStruct`, we can achieve a certain level of type stability.
Specifically, I made some modifications to `abstract_eval_new` to allow creating `PartialStruct` even for `:new` objects that are `!isconcretedispatch` (since `PartialStruct` can now represent abstract elements). I also adjusted `const_prop_argument_heuristic` to perform aggressive constant propagation using such `closure::PartialStruct`.

As a result, the following code now achieves type stability:
```julia
julia> Base.infer_return_type((Bool,Int,)) do b, y
           x = b ? 1 : missing
           inner = y -> x + y
           return inner(y)
       end
Any                   # master
Union{Missing, Int64} # this commit
```

However, this alone was not enough to fully resolve JuliaLang/julia#31909. The call graph of `map` is extremely complex, and simply applying constant propagation everywhere does not achieve the type safety requested in the issue.

Nevertheless this commit alone would still improve type stability for some cases, so I will go ahead and submit it as a PR.

---

inference"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-11-14T21:14:51Z","updated_at":"2024-11-15T10:16:40Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56565","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56565","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56565.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56565.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Inlining wants to know information about every isa_compileable_sig method as well as everything it might consider inlining (which is almost the same thing). So even if inference could bail on computing the type since it already reached the maximum fixed point, it should keep going to get that information. {"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56509","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56509/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56509/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56509/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56509","id":2645620846,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM6BYyg2","number":56509,"title":"WIP: Make world-age increments explicit","user":{"login":"Keno","id":1291671,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyOTE2NzE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1291671?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno","html_url":"https://github.com/Keno","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":12,"created_at":"2024-11-09T04:40:10Z","updated_at":"2024-11-18T11:25:51Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56509","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56509","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56509.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56509.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"This PR introduces a new, toplevel-only, syntax form `:worldinc` that semantically represents the effect of raising the current task's world age to the latest world for the remainder of the current toplevel evaluation (that context being an entry to `eval` or a module expression). For detailed motivation on why this is desirable, see #55145, which I won't repeat here, but the gist is that we never really defined when world-age increments and worse are inconsistent about it. This is something we need to figure out now, because the bindings partition work will make world age even more observable via bindings.

Having created a mechanism for world age increments, the big question is one of policy, i.e. when should these world age increments be inserted.

Several reasonable options exist:
1. After world-age affecting syntax constructs (as proprosed in #55145)
2. Option 1 + some reasonable additional cases that people rely on
3. Before any top level `call` expression
4. Before any expression at toplevel whatsover

As an example, case, consider `a == a` at toplevel. Depending on the semantics that could either be the same as in local scope, or each of the four world age dependent lookups (three binding lookups, one method lookup) could (potentially) occur in a different world age.

The general tradeoff here is between the risk of exposing the user to confusing world age errors and our ability to optimize top-level code (in general, any `:worldinc` statement will require us to fully pessimize or recompile all following code).

This PR basically implements option 2 with the following semantics:

1. The interpreter explicit raises the world age only at `:worldinc` exprs or after `:module` exprs.
2. The frontend inserts `:worldinc` after all struct definitions, method definitions, `using` and `import.
3. The `@eval` macro inserts a worldinc following the call to `eval` if at toplevel
4. A literal (syntactic) call to `include` gains an implicit `worldinc`.

Of these the fourth is probably the most questionable, but is necessary to make this non-breaking for most code patterns. Perhaps it would have been better to make `include` a macro from the beginning (esp because it already has semantics that look a little like reaching into the calling module), but that ship has sailed.

Unfortunately, I don't see any good intermediate options between this PR and option #3 above. I think option #3 is closest to what we have right now, but if we were to choose it and actually fix the soundness issues, I expect that we would be destroying all performance of global-scope code. For this reason, I would like to try to make the version in this PR work, even if the semantics are a little ugly.

The biggest pattern that this PR does not catch is:
```
eval(:(f() = 1))
f()
```

We could apply the same `include` special case to eval, but given the existence of `@eval` which allows addressing this at the macro level, I decided not to. We can decide which way we want to go on this based on what the package ecosystem looks like.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56509/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56509/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2482766532","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56509#issuecomment-2482766532","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56509","id":2482766532,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6T_ALE","user":{"login":"nanosoldier","id":14142636,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0MTQyNjM2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/14142636?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier","html_url":"https://github.com/nanosoldier","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-18T11:25:50Z","updated_at":"2024-11-18T11:25:50Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"The package evaluation job [you requested](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56509#issuecomment-2481875508) has completed - possible new issues were detected.

PR introduces a new, toplevel-only, syntax form `:worldinc` that semantically represents the effect of raising the current task's world age to the latest world for the remainder of the current For detailed motivation on why this is desirable, see #55145, which I won't repeat here, but the gist is that we never really defined when world-age increments and worse are inconsistent about it. This is something we need to figure out now, because the bindings partition work will make world age even more observable via bindings.

Having created a mechanism for world age increments, the big question is one of policy, i.e. when should these world age increments be inserted.

Several reasonable options exist:
1. After world-age affecting syntax constructs (as proprosed in #55145)
2. Option 1 + some reasonable additional cases that people rely on
3. Before any top level `call` expression
4. Before any expression at toplevel whatsover

As an example, case, consider `a == a` at toplevel. Depending on the semantics that could either be the same as in local scope, or each of the four world age dependent lookups (three binding lookups, one method lookup) could (potentially) occur in a different world age.

The general tradeoff here is between the risk of exposing the user to confusing world age errors and our ability to optimize top-level code (in general, any `:worldinc` statement will require us to fully pessimize or recompile all following code).

This PR basically implements option 2 with the following semantics:

1. The interpreter explicit raises the world age only at `:worldinc` exprs or after `:module` exprs.
2. The frontend inserts `:worldinc` after all struct definitions, method definitions, `using` and `import.
3. The `@eval` macro inserts a worldinc following the call to `eval` if at toplevel
4. A literal (syntactic) call to `include` gains an implicit `worldinc`.

Of these the fourth is probably the most questionable, but is necessary to make this non-breaking for most code patterns. Perhaps it would have been better to make `include` a macro from the beginning (esp because it already has semantics that look a little like reaching into the calling module), but that ship has sailed.

Unfortunately, I don't see any good intermediate options between this PR and option #3 above. I think option #3 is closest to what we have right now, but if we were to choose it and actually fix the soundness issues, I expect that we would be destroying all performance of global-scope code. For this reason, I would like to try to make the version in this PR work, even if the semantics are a little ugly.

The biggest pattern that this PR does not catch is:
```
eval(:(f() = 1))
f()
```

We could apply the same `include` special case to eval, but given the existence of `@eval` which allows addressing this at the macro level, I decided not to. We can decide which way we want to go on this based on what the package ecosystem looks like.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56509/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56509/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2484620523","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56509#issuecomment-2484620523","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56509","id":2484620523,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6UGEzr","user":{"login":"nanosoldier","id":14142636,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0MTQyNjM2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/14142636?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier","html_url":"https://github.com/nanosoldier","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nanosoldier/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-19T03:15:44Z","updated_at":"2024-11-19T03:15:44Z","author_association":"COLLABORATOR","body":"The package evaluation job [you requested](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56509#issuecomment-2484533826) has completed - possible new issues were detected.

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56687","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56687/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56687/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56687/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56687","id":2695296737,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM6DNyYa","number":56687,"title":"perform inference using optimizer-derived type information","user":{"login":"aviatesk","id":40514306,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwNTE0MzA2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/40514306?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk","html_url":"https://github.com/aviatesk","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-11-26T16:30:42Z","updated_at":"2024-11-26T16:48:07Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56687","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56687","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56687.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56687.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"In certain cases, the optimizer can introduce new type information. This is particularly evident in SROA, where load forwarding can reveal type information that was not visible during abstract interpretation. In such cases, re-running abstract interpretation using this new type information can be highly valuable, however, currently, this only occurs when semi-concrete interpretation happens to be triggered.

This commit introduces a new "post-optimization inference" phase at the end of the optimizer pipeline. When the optimizer derives new type information, this phase performs IR abstract interpretation to further optimize the IR.

Such cases are especially common in patterns involving captured
variables, as discussed in JuliaLang/julia#15276. By combining the
"post-optimization inference" implemented in this commit with EA-based
load forwarding, it is anticipated that the issue described in
JuliaLang/julia#15276 can largely be resolved.

inference using optimizer-derived type information","user":{"login":"aviatesk","id":40514306,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwNTE0MzA2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/40514306?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk","html_url":"https://github.com/aviatesk","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-11-26T16:30:42Z","updated_at":"2024-11-27T06:58:58Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56687","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56687","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56687.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56687.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"In certain cases, the optimizer can introduce new type information. This is particularly evident in SROA, where load forwarding can reveal type information that was not visible during abstract interpretation. In such cases, re-running abstract interpretation using this new type information can be highly valuable, however, currently, this only occurs when semi-concrete interpretation happens to be triggered.

This commit introduces a new "post-optimization inference" phase at the end of the optimizer pipeline. When the optimizer derives new type information, this phase performs IR abstract interpretation to further optimize the IR.

Such cases are especially common in patterns involving captured
variables, as discussed in JuliaLang/julia#15276. By combining the
"post-optimization inference" implemented in this commit with EA-based
load forwarding, it is anticipated that the issue described in
JuliaLang/julia#15276 can largely be resolved.

---

@nanosoldier `runbenchmarks("inference", vs=":master")`

allow EA-powered `finalizer` inlining","user":{"login":"aviatesk","id":40514306,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwNTE0MzA2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/40514306?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk","html_url":"https://github.com/aviatesk","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aviatesk/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-10-01T12:45:52Z","updated_at":"2024-10-01T13:44:22Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/55954","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55954","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55954.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55954.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"E.g. this allows `finalizer` inlining in the following case:
```julia
mutable struct ForeignBuffer{T}
    const ptr::Ptr{T}
end
const foreign_buffer_finalized = Ref(false)
function foreign_alloc(::Type{T}, length) where T
    ptr = Libc.malloc(sizeof(T) * length)
    ptr = Base.unsafe_convert(Ptr{T}, ptr)
    obj = ForeignBuffer{T}(ptr)
    return finalizer(obj) do obj
        Base.@assume_effects :notaskstate :nothrow
        foreign_buffer_finalized[] = true
        Libc.free(obj.ptr)
    end
end
function f_EA_finalizer(N::Int)
    workspace = foreign_alloc(Float64, N)
    GC.@preserve workspace begin
        (;ptr) = workspace
        Base.@assume_effects :nothrow @noinline println(devnull, "ptr = ", ptr)
    end
end
```
```julia
julia> @code_typed f_EA_finalizer(42)
CodeInfo(
1 ── %1  = Base.mul_int(8, N)::Int64
│    %2  = Core.lshr_int(%1, 63)::Int64
│    %3  = Core.trunc_int(Core.UInt8, %2)::UInt8
│    %4  = Core.eq_int(%3, 0x01)::Bool
└───       goto #3 if not %4
2 ──       invoke Core.throw_inexacterror(:convert::Symbol, UInt64::Type, %1::Int64)::Union{}
└───       unreachable
3 ──       goto #4
4 ── %9  = Core.bitcast(Core.UInt64, %1)::UInt64
└───       goto #5
5 ──       goto #6
6 ──       goto #7
7 ──       goto #8
8 ── %14 = $(Expr(:foreigncall, :(:malloc), Ptr{Nothing}, svec(UInt64), 0, :(:ccall), :(%9), :(%9)))::Ptr{Nothing}
└───       goto #9
9 ── %16 = Base.bitcast(Ptr{Float64}, %14)::Ptr{Float64}
│    %17 = %new(ForeignBuffer{Float64}, %16)::ForeignBuffer{Float64}
└───       goto #10
10 ─ %19 = $(Expr(:gc_preserve_begin, :(%17)))
│    %20 = Base.getfield(%17, :ptr)::Ptr{Float64}
│          invoke Main.println(Main.devnull::Base.DevNull, "ptr = "::String, %20::Ptr{Float64})::Nothing
│          $(Expr(:gc_preserve_end, :(%19)))
│    %23 = Main.foreign_buffer_finalized::Base.RefValue{Bool}
│          Base.setfield!(%23, :x, true)::Bool
│    %25 = Base.getfield(%17, :ptr)::Ptr{Float64}
│    %26 = Base.bitcast(Ptr{Nothing}, %25)::Ptr{Nothing}
│          $(Expr(:foreigncall, :(:free), Nothing, svec(Ptr{Nothing}), 0, :(:ccall), :(%26), :(%25)))::Nothing
└───       return nothing
) => Nothing
```

However, this is still a WIP. Before merging, I want to improve EA's precision a bit and at least fix the test case that is currently marked as `broken`. I also need to check its impact on compiler performance.

Additionally, I believe this feature is not yet practical. In particular, there is still significant room for improvement in the following areas:
- EA's interprocedural capabilities: currently EA is performed ad-hoc for limited frames because of latency reasons, which significantly reduces its precision in the presence of interprocedural calls.
- Relaxing the `:nothrow` check for finalizer inlining: the current algorithm requires `:nothrow`-ness on all paths from the allocation of the mutable struct to its last use, which is not practical for real-world cases. Even when `:nothrow` cannot be guaranteed, auxiliary optimizations such as inserting a `finalize` call after the last use might still be possible.

this allows `finalizer` inlining in the following case:
```julia
mutable struct ForeignBuffer{T}
    const ptr::Ptr{T}
end
const foreign_buffer_finalized = Ref(false)
function foreign_alloc(::Type{T}, length) where T
    ptr = Libc.malloc(sizeof(T) * length)
    ptr = Base.unsafe_convert(Ptr{T}, ptr)
    obj = ForeignBuffer{T}(ptr)
    return finalizer(obj) do obj
        Base.@assume_effects :notaskstate :nothrow
        foreign_buffer_finalized[] = true
        Libc.free(obj.ptr)
    end
end
function f_EA_finalizer(N::Int)
    workspace = foreign_alloc(Float64, N)
    GC.@preserve workspace begin
        (;ptr) = workspace
        Base.@assume_effects :nothrow @noinline println(devnull, "ptr = ", ptr)
    end
end
```
```julia
julia> @code_typed f_EA_finalizer(42)
CodeInfo(
1 ── %1  = Base.mul_int(8, N)::Int64
│    %2  = Core.lshr_int(%1, 63)::Int64
│    %3  = Core.trunc_int(Core.UInt8, %2)::UInt8
│    %4  = Core.eq_int(%3, 0x01)::Bool
└───       goto #3 if not %4
2 ──       invoke Core.throw_inexacterror(:convert::Symbol, UInt64::Type, %1::Int64)::Union{}
└───       unreachable
3 ──       goto #4
4 ── %9  = Core.bitcast(Core.UInt64, %1)::UInt64
└───       goto #5
5 ──       goto #6
6 ──       goto #7
7 ──       goto #8
8 ── %14 = $(Expr(:foreigncall, :(:malloc), Ptr{Nothing}, svec(UInt64), 0, :(:ccall), :(%9), :(%9)))::Ptr{Nothing}
└───       goto #9
9 ── %16 = Base.bitcast(Ptr{Float64}, %14)::Ptr{Float64}
│    %17 = %new(ForeignBuffer{Float64}, %16)::ForeignBuffer{Float64}
└───       goto #10
10 ─ %19 = $(Expr(:gc_preserve_begin, :(%17)))
│    %20 = Base.getfield(%17, :ptr)::Ptr{Float64}
│          invoke Main.println(Main.devnull::Base.DevNull, "ptr = "::String, %20::Ptr{Float64})::Nothing
│          $(Expr(:gc_preserve_end, :(%19)))
│    %23 = Main.foreign_buffer_finalized::Base.RefValue{Bool}
│          Base.setfield!(%23, :x, true)::Bool
│    %25 = Base.getfield(%17, :ptr)::Ptr{Float64}
│    %26 = Base.bitcast(Ptr{Nothing}, %25)::Ptr{Nothing}
│          $(Expr(:foreigncall, :(:free), Nothing, svec(Ptr{Nothing}), 0, :(:ccall), :(%26), :(%25)))::Nothing
└───       return nothing
) => Nothing
```

Additionally, I believe this feature is not yet practical. In particular, there is still significant room for improvement in the following areas:
- EA's interprocedural capabilities: currently EA is performed ad-hoc for limited frames because of latency reasons, which significantly reduces its precision in the presence of interprocedural calls.
- Relaxing the `:nothrow` check for finalizer inlining: the current algorithm requires `:nothrow`-ness on all paths from the allocation of the mutable struct to its last use, which is not practical for real-world cases. Even when `:nothrow` cannot be guaranteed, auxiliary optimizations such as inserting a `finalize` call after the last use might still be possible.
